files.content:
/**
 * Finance Routes
 * Defines API routes for financial data endpoints.
 * All routes are prefixed with /api when mounted in app.js.
 */

const express = require('express');
const router = express.Router();
const financeController = require('../controllers/financeController');

/**
 * GET /api/invoices
 * Fetches all invoices from FreshBooks.
 *
 * Query Parameters (optional, for future pagination support):
 *   - page: number (default: 1)
 *   - perPage: number (default: 15)
 *
 * Responses:
 *   200 - Invoice data retrieved successfully
 *   401 - FreshBooks authentication failure
 *   403 - Insufficient permissions
 *   429 - Rate limit exceeded
 *   500 - Server configuration or unexpected error
 *   502 - Network error reaching FreshBooks
 *   504 - Request timeout
 */
router.get('/invoices', financeController.getInvoices);

module.exports = router;

summary: Implemented FreshBooks API integration with three core files: (1) freshBooksApi.js service with fetchInvoices() that makes authenticated HTTPS requests to the FreshBooks API, normalizes invoice data, and handles all error cases (auth failures, network errors, timeouts, rate limits, malformed responses); (2) financeController.js with getInvoices() that calls the service and maps errors to appropriate HTTP status codes (401, 403, 429, 502, 504, 500); (3) financeRoutes.js defining GET /api/invoices route. Also included comprehensive unit tests covering success paths, all error scenarios, and the normalizeInvoice() helper function using mocked HTTPS requests.
